findingDetails.claim: The /prompt-improve save-location workflow discovers and creates legacy specs/ paths instead of the active .opencode/specs tracked packet root.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked prior registry entries for command-name, custom-path, and new-spec path findings; those cover stale /prompt and path sanitization, not the wrong root used by ordinary existing/new spec saves.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The command may intentionally support legacy specs/ folders, but it provides no .opencode/specs discovery or save path despite this active tracked packet using .opencode/specs.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the command is explicitly legacy-only and another documented prompt-save route owns .opencode/specs placement.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: wide parent-hub review scope with command, skill, agent, benchmark, and phased spec artifacts

targetSelection.selectionReason: correctness recycle pass over high-risk runtime entrypoints and prior-finding-adjacent path/command contracts

searchLedger.hypothesis: prompt-improve save paths can route artifacts outside active tracked packet roots

searchActions.result: setup uses ls -d specs/*/ and derives new folders as specs/[NNN]-[topic]/
save step repeats specs/[folder] and specs/[NNN]-[topic] for existing and new saves
tracked spec folders use .opencode/specs/[track]/[###-short-name]/; legacy specs/ may exist only as legacy

searchLedger.rationale: active command persistence paths only name the legacy root while the current tracked packet and convention use .opencode/specs

searchLedger.invariant: hub registry and router still expose prompt-improve and prompt-models as the only workflow modes

searchActions.result: hub routes by mode-registry and keeps two packet modes
registry contains prompt-improve and prompt-models with expected commands/tool surfaces
router signals point at both packet SKILL resources

searchLedger.rationale: no new router-shape correctness defect found in this pass
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: direct reads match the approved two-mode hub contract

searchLedger.invariant: new stale /prompt agent references should not be duplicated when already registered

searchActions.result: same stale runtime-agent references remain present and already mapped to R1-P1-001

searchLedger.rationale: re-raising would duplicate an active prior P1

searchLedger.hypothesis: prompt-models README may still omit active GLM-5.2 references in more than one place

searchActions.result: additional README model-set wording exists, but active-model README drift is already covered by R8-P2-001 and this pass prioritized a new command persistence P1

searchLedger.rationale: potential same-class README cleanup should be folded into existing R8-P2-001 remediation rather than raised as a new correctness finding here

title: /prompt-improve save-location workflow still discovers and creates legacy specs/ paths

detail: command save-location root conflicts with active .opencode/specs tracked packet convention

reason: hub SKILL, mode-registry, and hub-router still match the two workflow-mode contract
runtime-agent /prompt references remain covered by R1-P1-001
